title: The Darkness Before Them
author: Matthew Ward
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Darkness Before Them" book by Matthew Ward?
Options:
A. Still, there was a simple joy in coming and going freely, without worrying about attracting undue attention from an overbearing custodian, or contempt from a fireblood. On the second day after the Chainbreaker's departure, Kat found herself in Athenoch's small graveyard as they buried an old man, watching respectfully from a distance as friends and family placed pinwheel and black candle offerings without archons and shriversmen loitering nearby like vultures. She even caught sight of the bright glow of gloami-wisps flickering around the newer graves. Anywhere else, even those faint, rapidly dissipating ifrîti - the part of the soul that lingered longest after death - would have been captured and forced into servitude as lumani for whatever brief moments they had left.
B. However, it was a simple pleasure to go wherever she wanted, without fear of drawing too much notice from a strict caretaker, or disdain from a fireblood. Two days after the Chainbreaker had left, Kat was in Athenoch's small graveyard as an old man was buried, watching from a polite distance as friends and family put pinwheels and black candles on his grave without archons and shriversmen hovering nearby like vultures. She even saw the bright shimmer of gloami-wisps around the newer graves. Elsewhere, even those faint, quickly fading ifrîti - the part of the soul that lingered longest after death - would have been seized and forced into service as lumani for whatever brief time remained for them.
C. But for all of that, it was a simple joy to come and go as one pleased, without worrying about drawing the attention of an overzealous custodian, or a fireblood’s scorn. On the second afternoon following the  Chainbreaker ’s departure, Kat found herself in Athenoch’s tiny graveyard as an old man was laid to rest, and watched from respectful distance as friends and family placed offerings of pinwheels and black candles without archons and shriversmen lurking like vultures on the periphery. She even glimpsed the bright halo of gloami-wisps flickering about the fresher graves. Anywhere else, even those thin, rapidly dissipating ifrîti – the part of the soul that lingered longest after life had gone – would have been snared and indentured as lumani for what fading moments they possessed. 
D. But it was liberating to come and go freely, without worrying about attracting unwanted attention from an overbearing caretaker, or scorn from a fireblood. On the second day after the Chainbreaker's departure, Kat found herself in Athenoch's tiny graveyard as an old man was laid to rest, and she watched respectfully from a distance as friends and family placed pinwheel and black candle offerings without archons and shriversmen lurking like vultures nearby. She even caught a glimpse of the bright glow of gloami-wisps flickering around the newer graves. Anywhere else, even those thin, rapidly fading ifrîti - the part of the soul that lingered longest after death - would have been captured and enslaved as lumani for whatever fleeting moments they had left.
Answer:

She even caught sight of the bright glow of gloami-wisps flickering around the newer graves.
